name: noncompliance_posters
description: Notice of Non-Compliance (q.side_posters, Takehara Falls) end-to-end on Printmaker Mei: take the three moth prints, run the three gated paste buttons (attempt_one/two/three - the canvasser Kazuo stands ~127b away at 1940,105,2470 with sight range 10, so every attempt deterministically takes the unseen branch), then the clean turn-in (400 CD + 3x potion + 1x heal_ball + sq_posters_done). No prerequisite tags are assumed or faked; the preamble resets every chain tag and clears the reward items (including reward/standard exp candies).

steps.comment: -- reset (idempotent re-runs): chain tags + reward items + canvasser sight score --
defensive: zero any stale sight score so a frozen 1 can never fake a scold (errors if unloaded/unset - tolerated like the tag removes)
-- beat 1: the glue objection - take the three moth prints (sets sq_posters_started) --

expect_screen_text: Before the glue complaint — a civic observation. Two grey suits took the gym stairs this morning, briefcases matching, smiles matching. The mayor went up to meet them. He has not come down. Somebody with steadier nerves than a printmaker should go see about that.

Meanwhile, down here: Verified Trust, Verified Value, glued straight over my moth-wing prints. Bad glue. Three notices — gym entrance, falls overlook, bridge mural. I want my prints back on top, and I want the canvasser to never see it happen.

steps.wait_chat: Re-post the moth prints over three notices

steps.comment: -- beat 2: paste print one - gym entrance (attempt_one, unseen branch -> sq_poster_one) --

steps.expect_screen_text: Watch his loop from my counter

steps.click: Slip out and paste print one

steps.comment: -- beat 3: paste print two - falls overlook (attempt_two -> sq_poster_two) --
-- beat 4: paste print three - the bridge mural (attempt_three -> sq_poster_three) --
-- beat 5: clean turn-in (never scolded -> turn_in_clean entry, priority 32) --

expect_screen_text: Three prints up and he never saw a thing. Not once. You move like ink in water. The pulped slogans read verified trust, verified value — in a voice as flat as the paper deserved.
And under board three — you saw it. The old mural. The center figure painted out years ago. Nobody in town remembers commissioning either version. I checked the guild ledger. That page is missing.
